name: Housing Opportunities Of Beaver County, Incor
description_short: HUD-certified non-profit housing counselor in Beaver, Lawrence, and Mercer County, PA offering free pre-purchase counseling, foreclosure prevention, and homeownership assistance.
description_long:
Housing Opportunities Inc. (HOI) is a locally-based non-profit housing services organization operating across Beaver, Lawrence, and Mercer County, Pennsylvania. The organization is HUD-certified and functions as a program partner with USDA Rural Development, positioning it as an official resource within the federal affordable housing ecosystem. Founded to address declining housing trends and stabilize communities in Southwestern PA, HOI operates under a mission to help low and moderate-income families achieve and maintain homeownership.

HOI provides a comprehensive suite of free services including pre- and post-purchase housing counseling, financial planning, foreclosure prevention negotiation with lenders, down payment and closing cost assistance grants, low-interest loans for first-time homebuyers, and property rehabilitation guidance. They have expanded into affordable housing development, purchasing and rehabilitating distressed properties for resale to qualified buyers. The organization also operates a workforce development program called Housing Opportunities Workforce, offering hands-on training and job placement in construction and property maintenance. All counseling and advisory services are explicitly offered at no cost.

What distinguishes HOI is their dual approach combining financial counseling with direct property development and community revitalization. They have designed location-based closing cost assistance grants specifically for homes in HUD Qualified Census Tracts, partnered with the Pennsylvania Housing Finance Agency (PHFA) to expand this program. Their staff includes dedicated housing counselors, a development director, and workforce trainers, suggesting operational depth beyond basic counseling. The organization also participates in the distressed homeowner program available in only 18 Pennsylvania counties, positioning homeowners with a 30-day window after foreclosure notice to negotiate through HOI and the court.

HOI is genuinely positioned as a non-profit resource with no lending or credit repair component—they function as educators and advocates rather than creditors. The main caveat is geographic limitation: they serve only specific counties in Western PA and cannot assist consumers outside Beaver, Lawrence, and Mercer County. Additionally, while they offer grants and loan assistance, the website provides limited detail on eligibility criteria, income thresholds, or funding availability, requiring direct contact for specifics.
pros: HUD-certified housing counselor with official USDA Rural Development program partner status
All counseling and financial education services are completely free
Offers location-based closing cost assistance grants for homes in HUD Qualified Census Tracts
Provides foreclosure prevention negotiation with lenders within 30-day state program window
Operates as affordable housing developer, directly purchasing and rehabilitating properties for community revitalization
Includes workforce training and job placement program in construction and property maintenance
Evening appointments available beyond standard Monday-Friday 9am-4:45pm hours
Pre- and post-purchase counseling covers both acquisition and long-term homeowner success
cons: Geographic limitation: serves only Beaver, Lawrence, and Mercer County, PA—cannot assist out-of-region consumers
Website lacks specific details on income eligibility thresholds, grant amounts, or loan terms, requiring outbound inquiry
Limited publicly-available information on approval rates, average assistance amounts, or typical counseling outcomes
Foreclosure prevention program only applies to the 18-county PA distressed homeowner program—does not cover all foreclosure situations
No online application process or self-service tools visible; all engagement requires phone/email contact and scheduling
best_for: First-time homebuyers in Beaver, Lawrence, or Mercer County seeking free pre-purchase counseling and down payment assistance
Homeowners facing foreclosure in Pennsylvania's 18-county distressed program needing lender negotiation support
Low to moderate-income families qualifying for USDA Rural Development financing in eligible areas
Individuals seeking post-purchase counseling, mortgage troubleshooting, or financial planning related to homeownership
diagnosis: Best for low-to-moderate-income families in Beaver, Lawrence, or Mercer County, PA seeking free professional guidance on homeownership, down payment assistance, or foreclosure prevention. Critical caveat: geographic service area is strictly limited to those three Western PA counties; residents elsewhere will need to find local HUD-approved counseling agencies.
services: HUD-certified pre-purchase housing counseling and financial education
Post-purchase counseling and homeowner financial planning
Foreclosure prevention negotiation with lenders (PA 18-county distressed homeowner program)
Location-based closing cost assistance grants for HUD Qualified Census Tracts
Down payment and closing cost support for first-time homebuyers
Low-interest loan programs for home purchase and repairs
USDA Rural Development program partnership for qualified rural area financing
Property development and rehabilitation for affordable housing resale
Housing Opportunities Workforce training and job placement in construction and maintenance
Community housing initiatives and neighborhood revitalization projects
One-on-one housing counselor consultation (in-person, phone, evening appointments available)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 2418 Wilmington Rd, Suite C, New Castle, PA 16105
